name: Construction Prefabricated Buildings
description:
If you have a commercial, institutional or industrial building to put up and want it constructed as a prefabricated structure rather than built conventionally, the construction of prefabricated buildings is the service this page covers. SAMAN designs, fabricates, erects and hands over the finished building from our facilities in Bangalore and Greater Noida — you deal with one team from drawing to handover, not a chain of separate contractors. The build configured on this page is a 90 × 30 × 20 ft structure — 2,700 sq ft of floor area on a steel frame clad in PUF sandwich panels — constructed and handed over for ₹27,75,000 to ₹27,85,000, inclusive of all taxes.

A completed steel-blue prefabricated building on its plot — designed, built and handed over by SAMAN.

Which Buildings Can Be Constructed as Prefabricated Structures?

Most low- to mid-rise commercial, institutional and industrial buildings can be constructed as prefabricated structures: office blocks, showrooms and retail units, clinics and school blocks, site and administrative offices, staff facilities, and mid-size storage or light-production buildings. The structural frame and wall panels are factory-built, transported, and assembled on a prepared foundation — the same construction service applied to each building type.

Different prefabricated building types — office and light-industrial — built in sandstone-beige steel panels by SAMAN.

What changes from one building type to the next is the layout, the spans and the fit-out — not the underlying method. A single-storey office block is laid out for cabins, meeting rooms and a pantry; an institutional building such as a clinic or school block is planned around corridors and equal-sized rooms; a light-industrial or storage building is built for clear floor space and wider door openings. In every case the way we construct a prefabricated building follows the same sequence of factory fabrication and on-site assembly — only the drawing differs. If you want the underlying method explained in full before settling on a building type, our page on factory-built prefabricated construction sets out how the components are made and assembled.

What the Construction Service Covers — and Where Your Scope Begins

Across every building type the construction service covers the same defined scope. Our structural team designs the building to your floor plan and intended use, fabricates the steel frame and the wall and roof panels at our facility, transports the components to your site, and erects the building with our own crew — closing with doors, windows and the specified finishing, and a handover. You arrange two things on your side: a level plinth or foundation poured to the layout drawing we issue, and utility connections once the structure is handed over. Because that division of scope is fixed at quotation, a prefabricated building project carries no grey area over who is responsible for what.

SAMAN's crew erecting the steel frame and fixing slate-charcoal panels of a prefabricated building.

This page covers mid-scale buildings constructed as a standalone service. If your requirement is a large multi-bay factory or warehouse needing full structural documentation to IS codes and site coordination under a single contract, that is the wider turnkey PEB construction service rather than one building built to order.

The 90 × 30 ft Reference Build — Size, Materials and Price

The configuration priced on this page is a 90 × 30 × 20 ft building — 2,700 sq ft of floor area at a 20 ft eave height — constructed on a structural steel frame clad in PUF sandwich panels, and handed over for ₹27,75,000 to ₹27,85,000 inclusive of all taxes. That works out to roughly ₹1,028 per square foot for the constructed building. The figure covers the steel frame, the wall and roof panels, doors and windows as configured, transport to site, and erection by our own crew. It does not include your foundation or utility connections, which sit in your scope as set out above.

The 90 × 30 ft steel-blue reference build, complete on its plinth and handed over by SAMAN.

Other sizes follow the same construction service and are quoted per square foot, so a building larger or smaller than 2,700 sq ft is priced on the same basis rather than from a different rate card. Most buyers treat this 90 × 30 ft build as a reference point and adjust the floor area to their plot and use.

Building Across Types, or Building to One Fixed Specification?

Three nearby options serve slightly different needs, and choosing the right one saves time at quotation. This page is the right starting point when you know the building type you need constructed — an office, a commercial block, an institutional building — and want it designed and built to that use across whatever floor area suits your plot. If instead you want a single building set out end to end at one fixed configuration and price, our page on constructing a building to a set specification walks through one such build in full. And if you would rather select and purchase a building as a finished product line rather than commission a construction service, the range of prefab buildings is the better place to begin. Tell us the building type and the plot, and we will point you to the right one.

A burgundy-red prefabricated institutional building — clinic or school block — constructed by SAMAN.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does it cost to construct a prefabricated building?
The 90 × 30 × 20 ft (2,700 sq ft) steel-frame building on this page is constructed and handed over for ₹27,75,000 to ₹27,85,000 inclusive of taxes — about ₹1,028 per square foot for the finished structure. Larger or smaller buildings follow the same construction service and are quoted per square foot, with your foundation and utility connections costed separately.

Is this a construction service, or am I buying a finished building?
This is a construction service: whatever the building type — office, commercial or institutional — SAMAN designs, fabricates and erects it on your site and hands it over, rather than selling a fixed unit off a shelf. If you would prefer to choose and purchase a building from a ready product range, our prefab buildings pages are the place to start.

Can a prefabricated building be used as a permanent commercial or industrial structure?
Yes. The buildings are constructed on a structural steel frame with PUF sandwich panels — not temporary materials — so a prefabricated building serves as a permanent office, commercial or institutional structure with the working-life expectations of a conventional low-rise building, while going up in a fraction of the time.

Get a Construction Quote

Share your plot location, building type, floor area and intended use, and our team will issue a layout and price.
Bengaluru — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
